mechanism_narrative: A 5% YoY increase in real GDP growth rate over the next 60 trading days signals a strengthening labor market, leading to higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if the 20-day forward return is not significantly higher than the S&P 500's historical average during similar late-cycle expansions (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% increase in the unemployment rate over the next 5 trading days signals a tightening labor market, leading to higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if the 20-day forward return is not significantly higher than the S&P 500's historical average during periods of rising unemployment (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 10% increase in industrial production over the next 20 trading days signals a strengthening supply chain, leading to higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if the 20-day forward return is not significantly higher than the S&P 500's historical average during similar early-cycle expansions (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 50-basis-point decrease in the federal funds rate over the next 60 trading days signals a easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if the 20-day forward return is not significantly higher than the S&P 500's historical average during periods of easing monetary policy (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 10% increase in housing starts over the next 20 trading days signals a strengthening housing market, leading to higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if the 20-day forward return is not significantly higher than the S&P 500's historical average during similar late-cycle expansions (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% decrease in the unemployment rate over the next 5 trading days signals a tightening labor market, leading to higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if the 20-day forward return is not significantly higher than the S&P 500's historical average during periods of falling unemployment (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 10% decrease in industrial production over the next 20 trading days signals a weakening supply chain, leading to lower corporate earnings and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if the 20-day forward return is not significantly lower than the S&P 500's historical average during similar early-cycle contractions (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 10% increase in real GDP growth rate over the next 60 trading days signals a strengthening labor market, leading to higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns. This effect is amplified when the federal funds rate is below 2%.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with accommodative monetary policy
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if the 20-day forward return is not significantly higher than the S&P 500's historical average during similar late-cycle expansions with accommodative monetary policy (t-test p < 0.05).
